_description: Extension schema for r/covidlonghaulers. Activates 7 base-optional fields and adds 7 COVID-specific extension fields.

covid_wave.description: Which COVID variant or wave the patient contracted

infection_count.description: How many times the patient has been infected with COVID

vaccination_status.description: COVID vaccination status including doses and brand

long_covid_duration_months.description: How long the patient has had Long COVID specifically (in months)

functional_status_tier.description: Functional capacity level with PEM awareness (housebound, bedbound, mild, moderate, severe)

biomarker_results.description: Lab values, biomarkers, or test results mentioned

clinical_trial_participation.description: Participation or exclusion from clinical trials

neuromotor_manifestations.description: Motor symptoms including tremors, fasciculations/twitches, weakness in specific limbs, gait disturbance, and coordination problems that develop or progress post-infection.
neuromotor_manifestations.research_value: Progressive neuromotor symptoms post-infection help characterize whether presentation aligns with demyelination, motor neuron involvement, or central nervous system dysfunction.

self_harm_behaviors.description: Specific self-injurious or compulsive behaviors patients report engaging in, including nail biting, skin picking, hair pulling, and cutting. Important for understanding behavioral manifestations of underlying conditions and assessing severity/impact.
self_harm_behaviors.research_value: Researchers need to track specific self-harm behaviors and their triggers to understand psychiatric comorbidities and treatment efficacy for behavioral compulsions.

normal_test_results_despite_symptoms.description: Instances where standard diagnostic tests (blood work, imaging, endoscopy) return normal results despite the patient experiencing significant symptoms. This pattern is critical for understanding diagnostic gaps and potential novel disease mechanisms.
normal_test_results_despite_symptoms.research_value: Identifies patients who may have atypical presentations or novel pathophysiology not captured by standard diagnostic approaches, informing development of new diagnostic criteria.

vaccine_adverse_events.description: Specific adverse events experienced after vaccination, particularly post-mRNA vaccine. Captures temporal relationship between vaccine administration and symptom onset, which is critical for safety monitoring and pharmacovigilance research.
vaccine_adverse_events.research_value: Essential for post-market vaccine safety surveillance and understanding the spectrum of adverse events that may be under-reported in official systems.

autonomic_dysregulation_symptoms.description: Symptoms suggesting autonomic nervous system dysfunction including orthostatic issues, tachycardia, adrenaline surges, breathlessness at rest, and restless sensations despite fatigue.
autonomic_dysregulation_symptoms.research_value: Autonomic symptoms are under-recognized in post-viral illness; mapping these helps identify dysautonomia as a treatable component.

symptom_triggers.description: Specific activities, states, or exposures that reliably trigger or worsen symptoms (e.g., fatigue, overexertion, lack of sleep, heat, specific foods). Distinct from general onset triggers—this captures ongoing triggers for flares.
symptom_triggers.research_value: Understanding symptom triggers is essential for management strategies and identifying patterns in dysautonomic and post-viral conditions.

supplement_and_nootropic_use.description: Specific supplements, nootropics, or cognitive enhancers patients use or are investigating, including doses and combinations. Distinct from general alternative_treatments as it captures self-directed biochemical intervention attempts.
supplement_and_nootropic_use.research_value: Tracking specific nootropic and supplement combinations helps identify common self-treatment patterns and potential adverse interactions in chronic illness populations.

treatment_failure_history.description: Pattern of multiple failed treatment attempts before finding something that works, including duration of trial-and-error period and number of unsuccessful interventions attempted.
treatment_failure_history.research_value: Understanding treatment failure patterns helps identify treatment-resistant phenotypes and informs decision-making about when to escalate to more intensive interventions.

cognitive_impairment_type.description: Specific types of cognitive dysfunction reported, including brain fog, memory loss (names/faces), concentration problems, and derealization. Important for characterizing neurological manifestations across conditions like long COVID and ME/CFS.
cognitive_impairment_type.research_value: Researchers need to distinguish specific cognitive deficits to understand neurological phenotypes and track cognitive recovery patterns.

neuropathic_pain_distribution.description: Patterns of neuropathic symptoms including peripheral neuropathy, nerve pain, burning sensations, and localized musculoskeletal pain with specific anatomical distributions.
neuropathic_pain_distribution.research_value: Neuropathic pain patterns and distributions help differentiate post-viral neuropathy from other etiologies and track treatment response.

compound_pharmacy_use.description: Use of compounded medications from specialty pharmacies, including which pharmacies, which compounds, dosing regimens, and reported efficacy. Indicates patient engagement with non-FDA-standard treatment approaches.
compound_pharmacy_use.research_value: Compounded medication use indicates treatment at the margins of standard care and should be tracked separately to assess safety, efficacy, and dosing variability in chronic disease populations.

flare_recovery_pattern.description: The cyclical pattern of flare onset, duration, and return to baseline that characterizes some chronic illnesses, particularly including recovery timelines and whether baseline improves or worsens with each cycle
flare_recovery_pattern.research_value: Understanding flare-remission cycles helps researchers predict disease trajectories and identify whether treatments improve recovery times or stabilize baseline function

drug_induced_deterioration.description: Medications or specific drugs that directly caused or significantly worsened symptoms, often with a clear temporal relationship and quantifiable change in function. This captures iatrogenic harm that patients need to document and researchers need to track.
drug_induced_deterioration.research_value: Critical for pharmacovigilance and understanding adverse drug reactions in neuropathy populations; helps identify which medications cause iatrogenic harm.

bulbar_symptoms.description: Speech and swallowing difficulties including hoarseness, voice changes, difficulty with speech effort, and dysphagia (food/liquid lingering in throat).
bulbar_symptoms.research_value: Bulbar symptoms in post-viral illness raise concern for motor neuron or brainstem involvement; tracking resolution helps distinguish functional from structural causes.

viral_trigger_flares.description: Specific viral infections (rhinovirus, cold, flu, COVID) that trigger or exacerbate chronic illness symptoms, particularly relevant for long COVID and dysautonomia patients who experience relapse patterns after viral exposure
viral_trigger_flares.research_value: Researchers studying long COVID and dysautonomia need to track which viral infections reliably trigger relapses to understand immune mechanisms and predict flare patterns

sleep_architecture_problems.description: Non-standard sleep issues beyond simple insomnia/hypersomnia: early morning awakening, unrefreshing sleep despite long duration, fragmented sleep (waking after 3 hours), sleep need changes post-illness.
sleep_architecture_problems.research_value: Sleep architecture disruption is a core feature of post-viral syndromes; tracking specific patterns helps differentiate pathophysiology from primary sleep disorders.

dysautonomia_nervous_system_subtype.description: Specific autonomic nervous system dysfunction patterns reported by patients, such as sympathetic overdrive, parasympathetic dysfunction, or mixed presentation that patients identify as their dysautonomia type
dysautonomia_nervous_system_subtype.research_value: Identifying specific dysautonomia subtypes enables targeted research on autonomic mechanisms and helps phenotype patients for treatment studies

post_exertional_malaise_pattern.description: Worsening of symptoms following physical or cognitive activity, including fatigue disproportionate to exertion level and delayed symptom onset after activity.
post_exertional_malaise_pattern.research_value: Post-exertional malaise is a core diagnostic criterion in ME/CFS; characterizing exertion thresholds and symptom trajectories is essential for treatment planning.

symptom_onset_pattern.description: Acute vs. gradual symptom onset, single acute event vs. recurrent episodes, and timing relationships between initial infection and symptom manifestation.
symptom_onset_pattern.research_value: Distinguishing acute vs. recurrent vs. progressive onsets helps identify different post-viral illness mechanisms and may predict treatment response.

cognitive_symptoms.description: Specific cognitive or neurological symptoms including brain fog, mental clarity issues, memory problems, and mental processing difficulties
cognitive_symptoms.research_value: Brain fog and cognitive dysfunction are hallmark Long COVID symptoms that deserve specific tracking separate from general conditions

symptom_location_spreading.description: Documented cases where chronic symptoms expand to new anatomical locations over time or during flares, such as sensory symptoms migrating from hands/feet to upper thighs or new body regions being affected
symptom_location_spreading.research_value: Documenting symptom migration patterns helps understand disease progression mechanisms and whether expanding symptoms indicate worsening disease or normal symptom variation

comorbid_condition_relationships.description: Documented or suspected overlaps and causal relationships between multiple concurrent conditions (e.g., MCAS potentially explaining PEM, dysautonomia/POTS co-occurrence with ME/CFS or Long COVID).
comorbid_condition_relationships.research_value: Identifies clinically relevant comorbidity patterns and potential mechanistic links that could inform unified disease models.

energy_and_fatigue_patterns.description: Specific patterns of energy availability, fatigue onset, crash cycles, diurnal variation, or reliance on stimulants to maintain function. Distinct from general activity_level as it captures dynamic energy fluctuations.
energy_and_fatigue_patterns.research_value: Energy and fatigue patterns are core to understanding post-exertional malaise, circadian dysfunction, and mitochondrial disease phenotypes in chronic illness.

medication_side_effect_profile.description: Specific adverse effects experienced from medications, including dose-dependent changes, onset timing, and reversibility. Tracks tolerability beyond standard adverse event lists.
medication_side_effect_profile.research_value: Detailed side effect tracking enables researchers to model dose-response relationships and identify reversible versus persistent adverse effects in MCAS populations.

pharmacy_or_compounding_source.description: Specific pharmacies, compounding facilities, or practitioners patients utilize for obtaining medications or compounds, particularly specialty and mail-order pharmacies.
pharmacy_or_compounding_source.research_value: Tracking which pharmacies and compounders patients use helps identify gaps in access, quality variation, and emerging sources for specialized treatments.

cognitive_complaints.description: Patient-reported difficulties with concentration, focus, mental clarity, memory, or executive function. Common in chronic illness and important for assessing disease burden and treatment effects.
cognitive_complaints.research_value: Cognitive complaints are prevalent in chronic illness and post-viral conditions and should be systematically assessed as a distinct symptom domain.

post_covid_neurological_complications.description: Acute and chronic neurological complications occurring after COVID-19 infection, including stroke, cognitive symptoms, and other CNS manifestations. Important for understanding post-COVID sequelae mechanisms.
post_covid_neurological_complications.research_value: Helps characterize the spectrum of post-COVID neurological complications and their temporal relationship to infection, informing long-COVID pathophysiology research.

supplement_efficacy.description: Specific supplements reported as beneficial by patients, with subjective effectiveness and mechanism. Captures self-managed interventions between pharmaceutical options.
supplement_efficacy.research_value: Cataloging supplement use helps researchers understand patient-driven management strategies and identify potentially beneficial adjunctive therapies for MCAS.

exercise_induced_symptoms.description: Specific adverse reactions or symptom exacerbation triggered by physical activity or exercise, including post-exertional malaise (PEM), insomnia after workouts, or sympathetic nervous system activation
exercise_induced_symptoms.research_value: Exercise intolerance and PEM are hallmark features of conditions like ME/CFS and require specific tracking separate from general activity level, as they represent pathological responses to exertion rather than deconditioning.

peptide_treatments.description: Use of peptide therapies (including specific named peptides like Tirzep, Urolithin A) as treatment interventions, including subjective response patterns
peptide_treatments.research_value: Peptide therapies represent an emerging treatment class in chronic illness communities; tracking specific peptides and their subjective outcomes is important for understanding off-label treatment adoption and efficacy patterns.

genital_skin_manifestations.description: Post-viral skin changes in genital area: scaling, flaking, erythema, with cyclical patterns. May occur independently of sexually transmitted infections.
genital_skin_manifestations.research_value: Genital dermatitis following viral infection may reflect immune dysregulation or viral persistence; documenting these manifestations helps identify systemic post-viral phenotypes.

functional_decline_trajectory.description: Pattern of progressive functional loss over months/years from initial infection: transition from independent to housebound to bedbound to loss of basic self-care ability. Captures disease progression.
functional_decline_trajectory.research_value: Disease trajectory (improving, stable, progressive) is a critical outcome variable for prognosis and treatment evaluation in post-viral illness; progressive functional decline predicts worse prognosis.

negative_test_results_pattern.description: Tests that returned normal or negative despite active symptoms. Captures the gap between clinical presentation and objective findings, particularly relevant for small fiber neuropathy and other conditions with normal standard testing.
negative_test_results_pattern.research_value: Researchers need to identify cases where standard diagnostics fail to capture disease, particularly for small fiber neuropathy and other conditions with normal conventional labs.

functional_disability_trajectory.description: Specific loss of physical function with timeline (e.g., limb non-usability, loss of ambulation), distinct from general 'activity_level' by capturing acute or progressive disability milestones.
functional_disability_trajectory.research_value: Quantifies disease progression and treatment-related disability to assess clinical severity and prognosis.

post_exertional_malaise_severity.description: Exaggerated fatigue or symptom worsening following minimal physical activity, with recovery time. Includes patients reporting inability to perform basic self-care (bathing, eating) or predicting functional decline.
post_exertional_malaise_severity.research_value: Post-exertional malaise is a cardinal feature of long COVID and ME/CFS; severity and activity threshold are critical for prognosis and for distinguishing from other conditions.

symptom_triggers_environmental.description: External environmental factors that trigger or exacerbate symptoms, including weather conditions, course conditions, and seasonal patterns. This is distinct from general symptom_triggers as it focuses on environmental/weather-based triggers rather than activity or food.
symptom_triggers_environmental.research_value: Understanding how environmental factors modulate disease/functional limitation severity helps identify patterns in condition trajectories and environmental management strategies.

unrefreshing_sleep.description: Sleep that does not restore energy despite adequate duration. Patients report waking up tired or sleeping excessively without feeling rested. Distinct from sleep quantity.
unrefreshing_sleep.research_value: Non-restorative sleep is a hallmark symptom in post-viral conditions and chronic fatigue; tracking this separately from sleep duration helps identify specific sleep architecture problems requiring investigation.

immunosuppressive_medication_use.description: Use of immunosuppressive or immunomodulatory medications in treatment of post-vaccine or post-COVID complications. Captures specific medications and outcomes.
immunosuppressive_medication_use.research_value: Helps identify which immunological interventions patients report as beneficial, informing treatment protocols for post-vaccine/post-COVID inflammatory conditions.

mucosal_dryness_pattern.description: Symptoms of dry mucous membranes including dry mouth, dry lips/mucous membranes, indicating possible autoimmune or systemic involvement. Often part of Sjögren's-like patterns or systemic inflammatory conditions.
mucosal_dryness_pattern.research_value: Clustering of mucosal dryness suggests possible autoimmune conditions like Sjögren's or systemic inflammatory response.

performance_variability_pattern.description: Patterns of extreme inconsistency in functional capacity or symptom severity with no clear trigger - good days alternating unpredictably with severe symptom days. This captures the specific pattern of high variability rather than just symptom severity.
performance_variability_pattern.research_value: Quantifying the degree and pattern of functional variability helps researchers identify whether this represents disease instability, post-exertional malaise, or metabolic fluctuation.

persistent_symptoms_unresponsive_to_trea.description: Patterns of symptoms that persist despite multiple medication trials or interventions. Indicates treatment-resistant disease requiring investigation.
persistent_symptoms_unresponsive_to_trea.research_value: Identifies subgroups with treatment-resistant illness requiring novel therapeutic approaches and mechanistic investigation.
patterns: \b(?:pain|symptom|headache|pressure|burning|fatigue|nausea|dizziness|rash|swelling|fever|ache|discomfort|bleeding|cramping|numbness|tingling)s?\b[^.]{0,60}\b(?:has|have|that has|which has|still|never)\b[^.]{0,40}\b(?:not gone away|not going away|not resolv\w+|not improv\w+|not better|persists?|persisting|persistent|remain\w+|continu\w+|won't go away|wont go away|keeps? coming back|never ends?|never ending|never gone|still there|still present|still here)

\b(?:tried|taking|on|using|been on|started)\b[^.]{0,80}\b(?:nothing|none of (?:it|them|the meds?|the medications?)|not (?:working|helping|doing anything)|(?:doesn't|don't|didn't|hasn't|haven't|not) (?:work|help|do anything|touch it|make (?:a )?(?:difference|dent)))

autonomic_dysfunction_features.description: Specific autonomic nervous system manifestations including heart racing, palpitations, shakiness, adrenaline surges, temperature dysregulation, and fear-like autonomic attacks.
autonomic_dysfunction_features.research_value: Detailed autonomic symptom profiles are critical for understanding dysautonomia subtypes and guiding neurological vs. cardiac workup.

treatment_trial_experimentation.description: Patient attempts to self-manage or self-treat conditions through trying multiple approaches, interventions, or lifestyle modifications to determine efficacy.
treatment_trial_experimentation.research_value: Understanding what treatments patients self-trial and their perceived efficacy helps identify gaps in conventional care and patient information needs.

demographic_recovery_context.description: Age and baseline health status relative to illness onset, contextualizing severity expectations and recovery prognosis in young, previously healthy populations.
demographic_recovery_context.research_value: Contextualizes disease severity and recovery expectations in populations with high prognostic variability based on age and baseline health.

post_viral_illness_phenotype.description: Clustering of conditions recognized as post-viral sequelae (ME/CFS, Long COVID, POTS, dysautonomia) that share phenotypic features and mechanisms, distinguishing them from baseline chronic illness.
post_viral_illness_phenotype.research_value: Enables researchers to study the umbrella category of post-viral syndromes as a disease class with shared mechanisms.

functional_impact_domains.description: Specific life domains affected by illness (work capacity, cognitive function, social participation, academic engagement) and degree of functional impairment.
functional_impact_domains.research_value: Tracks functional capacity evolution across disease domains to quantify quality of life impact and rehabilitation outcomes.

research_participation_barriers.description: Systemic and individual barriers to patient involvement in clinical research, advocacy, and clinical trials—including physical limitations, emotional burden, and volunteer retention challenges.
research_participation_barriers.research_value: Identifies systemic factors limiting patient-engaged research and informs recruitment/retention strategies for ME/CFS clinical studies.

etiological_uncertainty_post_vaccine_vs_.description: Patient uncertainty or questioning about whether symptoms are attributable to COVID infection versus vaccine, particularly in temporal proximity. Captures diagnostic confusion common in post-pandemic era.
etiological_uncertainty_post_vaccine_vs_.research_value: Essential for understanding attribution patterns and temporal relationships in post-vaccine and post-COVID illness, critical for establishing causality in research.
patterns: \b(?:not sure|unsure|wondering|question|think|believe|suspect|maybe|possibly|could be|might be|could have been|was it|is it|don.t know).{0,80}\b(?:vaccine|vax|shot|jab|covid|infection|virus).{0,80}\b(?:caused|triggered|gave|led to|or|vs\.?|versus)\b

biomarker_levels.description: Specific measurable biomarker values and their reference ranges that patients have been tested for, enabling comparison of quantitative test results across populations. Critical for understanding disease severity and diagnostic criteria variance.
biomarker_levels.research_value: Allows researchers to establish distribution of biomarker values in patient populations and identify thresholds for diagnostic or severity classification.

multiple_concurrent_autoimmune_diseases.description: Diagnosis of multiple autoimmune or inflammatory conditions simultaneously or within short timeframe, suggesting autoimmune clustering or cross-reactive etiology.
multiple_concurrent_autoimmune_diseases.research_value: Identifies potential autoimmune disease clustering patterns that may indicate shared etiology or cross-reactive mechanisms.
patterns: \b(?:i\s+(?:also\s+)?have|also\s+(?:have|diagnosed|dx))\s+(?:[\w\s]+?(?:itis|osis|pathy|emia|syndrome|disease|disorder))\b(?:[\w\s,]+)?\b(?:in addition to|on top of|along with|as well as)\b

senescent_cell_targeting.description: Patient interest in or trial of compounds targeting senescent (zombie) cells, including senolytics like fisetin, dasatinib+quercetin combinations, navitoclax, and ABT-737.
senescent_cell_targeting.research_value: Senolytic therapy represents an emerging mechanistic treatment for post-viral cognitive dysfunction; tracking patient use and perceived efficacy helps inform clinical trial design.

comorbid_joint_pain_phenotype.description: Patients with arthritis in specific joints (particularly DIP joints of fingers, wrist) that complicates fine motor tasks and requires equipment modifications.
comorbid_joint_pain_phenotype.research_value: Understanding the prevalence and pattern of joint involvement in ME/CFS and Long COVID populations could inform whether this represents part of the disease pathology or secondary deconditioning.

psychogenic_skepticism_experience.description: Patient experiences with medical dismissal through psychogenic/mass hysteria framing, and their reactions to scientific refutation of such claims.
psychogenic_skepticism_experience.research_value: Understanding how often and how severely patients experience psychogenic framing helps quantify iatrogenic harm and medical gaslighting in ME/CFS and Long COVID populations.

adverse_effect_tolerance.description: How patients describe their tolerance or acceptance of adverse effects from treatments, including willingness to tolerate severe side effects for perceived benefit.
adverse_effect_tolerance.research_value: Understanding adverse effect tolerance helps explain treatment adherence patterns and willingness to pursue aggressive or unconventional interventions.

negative_biopsy_positive_other_tests.description: Pattern where skin biopsy (standard diagnostic test) is negative but other objective tests (quantitative sensory testing, serology, antibody panels) are positive, indicating diagnostic discordance and potential for missed diagnosis.
negative_biopsy_positive_other_tests.research_value: Identifies diagnostic gaps where standard biopsy fails to detect disease confirmed by alternative testing; important for refining diagnostic criteria.

heat_sensitivity.description: Specific reactions to heat exposure, including temperature thresholds that trigger symptom exacerbation. Relevant for MCAS and post-COVID patients who report heat as a potent activator.
heat_sensitivity.research_value: Heat sensitivity is a critical environmental trigger that influences lifestyle and treatment options (e.g., hyperthermia feasibility) in MCAS patients.

supplement_adjuncts_for_symptom_manageme.description: Specific supplements or nutraceuticals used to manage symptoms, including magnesium glycinate for sleep, ashwagandha and L-theanine for anxiety/stress, and other targeted supplements.
supplement_adjuncts_for_symptom_manageme.research_value: Documenting supplement stacks and perceived efficacy helps identify low-risk adjunctive symptom management strategies and generates hypotheses for mechanistic studies.

geographic_treatment_availability.description: Documentation of which geographic regions provide or deny specific treatments for a condition, highlighting disparities in access to specialized care (e.g., IVIG, expertise in specific diseases).
geographic_treatment_availability.research_value: Maps healthcare access inequities and identifies where specialized treatments for rare neuropathies are available vs. unavailable.

post_exertional_malaise_triggers.description: Specific activities, exertion levels, or situations that trigger or worsen post-exertional malaise (PEM) in ME/CFS and Long COVID patients. Captures what types of physical or cognitive activity patients must avoid or limit.
post_exertional_malaise_triggers.research_value: Researchers need to understand what specific activities trigger or exacerbate PEM to design safer rehabilitation protocols and activity pacing strategies for ME/CFS and Long COVID populations.

mast_cell_trigger_foods.description: Specific foods or food categories that trigger mast cell activation symptoms in patients, often requiring elimination diets. Important for understanding dietary management in MCAS and post-COVID patients.
mast_cell_trigger_foods.research_value: Understanding trigger foods helps researchers identify dietary patterns that differentiate MCAS phenotypes and predict symptom flares.

medication_triggered_symptom_onset.description: Specific medications or exposures that triggered or significantly worsened the onset of the primary condition. Distinguishes drug-induced from idiopathic disease onset.
medication_triggered_symptom_onset.research_value: Identifying medication-triggered disease onset helps researchers understand iatrogenic MCAS etiology and predict adverse reactions in treatment-naive patients.

treatment_failure_pattern.description: Pattern where standard first-line treatments for a symptom paradoxically trigger or worsen mast cell symptoms, necessitating alternative approaches.
treatment_failure_pattern.research_value: Understanding treatment-induced paradoxical worsening helps researchers identify MCAS-specific contraindications and develop safer therapeutic algorithms.

behavioral_triggers.description: Specific emotions, situations, or states that precipitate symptom flares or harmful behaviors, such as anger, stress, fatigue, or boredom. Critical for understanding behavior management and relapse prevention.
behavioral_triggers.research_value: Understanding behavioral triggers enables targeted interventions and helps identify whether conditions are emotionally driven versus physiologically driven.

test_results_discordance.description: Situations where objective test results (blood work, imaging, EMG, MRI) are normal or inconclusive despite significant symptoms, creating diagnostic uncertainty and validation challenges.
test_results_discordance.research_value: Documenting normal test results despite active symptoms helps identify cases where pathology may not be detected by standard diagnostics.

specialist_consultation_pattern.description: Multi-specialist diagnostic workup pattern showing which specialists were consulted during diagnostic odyssey (endocrinologist, nephrologist, gynecologist, etc.).
specialist_consultation_pattern.research_value: Documenting specialist consultation patterns reveals common diagnostic pathways and helps identify which specialists successfully recognize MCAS versus those who miss it.

cognitive_symptom_patterns.description: Specific cognitive symptoms (brain fog, difficulty concentrating, memory issues) and what reduces or exacerbates them. Includes observations about attention-demanding tasks and mental fatigue.
cognitive_symptom_patterns.research_value: Brain fog is a major cognitive symptom in long COVID; tracking improvement mechanisms and triggers could guide cognitive rehabilitation strategies.

treatment_response_timeline.description: Expected timeframe for treatment efficacy, including minimum duration needed before assessing benefit versus side effect emergence timing.
treatment_response_timeline.research_value: Understanding expected response timelines helps researchers design adequate trial durations and helps patients distinguish reversible early side effects from true treatment failure.

cognitive_coping_strategies.description: Specific mental/cognitive techniques patients develop or adopt to manage symptoms, including mindfulness practices, attentional redirection, and present-moment focus techniques adapted for chronic illness.
cognitive_coping_strategies.research_value: Patients develop evidence-informed coping strategies for managing complex chronic illness symptoms; documenting these could inform psychosocial and mind-body interventions.

symptom_improvement_tracking.description: Patients' self-directed experimentation with lifestyle changes or supplements and their systematic observation of symptom changes (improvement or return of symptoms after stopping). This captures the detailed before/after patterns patients document themselves.
symptom_improvement_tracking.research_value: Captures patient-driven hypothesis testing and symptom tracking that could inform real-world evidence studies on dietary and lifestyle interventions.

symptom_interconnection_patterns.description: Observations of how multiple symptoms appear linked or cascade from a single trigger (e.g., dehydration → low electrolytes → palpitations → anxiety → sleep disruption → worsening palpitations). Captures multi-system symptom chains.
symptom_interconnection_patterns.research_value: Understanding symptom interconnection is critical for designing holistic treatment approaches and identifying leverage points for intervention.

activity_tolerance_limits.description: Specific, documented thresholds at which patients experience symptom exacerbation (particularly post-exertional malaise triggers or exercise tolerance limits). Includes specific timeframes, activities, and physiological responses.
activity_tolerance_limits.research_value: Researchers need specific activity tolerance thresholds to understand post-exertional malaise patterns and design appropriate rehabilitation protocols.

healthcare_coordination_gaps.description: Missed diagnostic connections, lack of information sharing between providers, failure to integrate patient's disease history into treatment decisions. Captures systemic failures in multi-disciplinary care coordination.
healthcare_coordination_gaps.research_value: Healthcare system failures in chronic complex illness are barriers to correct diagnosis; documenting gaps helps identify opportunities for care coordination improvements.

oral_mucosal_manifestations.description: Visible changes in mouth/throat tissues appearing after viral infection: white coating on tongue, white tissue in throat, oral sores, fordyce spots. Often temporally linked to disease onset.
oral_mucosal_manifestations.research_value: Oral manifestations in post-COVID patients may indicate viral persistence or immune dysregulation; tracking specific mucosal changes helps identify viral and inflammatory phenotypes.

symptom_type_distribution.description: The specific mix of different sensory symptoms reported (numbness, burning, zapping pain, weakness) and which predominates. Different neuropathies present with different symptom phenotypes.
symptom_type_distribution.research_value: Symptom phenotype helps classify neuropathy subtypes and predict treatment responsiveness.

comorbid_structural_complications.description: Development of new structural/mechanical complications (hernias, other conditions) that may be triggered or exacerbated by the chronic illness or its sequelae (deconditioning, reduced activity).
comorbid_structural_complications.research_value: Chronic illness complications (secondary to deconditioning, reduced activity tolerance) need systematic tracking to understand the full disease burden and complication rates.

electrolyte_and_cardiac_symptom_clusters.description: Co-occurrence of low potassium/electrolyte abnormalities with cardiac symptoms (palpitations, PACs, arrhythmias) and the temporal relationship between correction/worsening and symptom changes.
electrolyte_and_cardiac_symptom_clusters.research_value: In chronic illness like long COVID, cardiac arrhythmias may have multifactorial causes (electrolytes, dehydration, stress, GERD); researchers need to understand which interventions target which mechanisms.

gastrointestinal_bacterial_overgrowth.description: Documentation of SIBO (small intestinal bacterial overgrowth) testing and treatment, including severity and treatment outcomes. Captures post-viral GI complications requiring specific diagnosis.
gastrointestinal_bacterial_overgrowth.research_value: SIBO following COVID-19 is an emerging post-viral complication; tracking this helps characterize microbiome dysbiosis as a potential mechanism of long COVID.

treatment_efficacy_for_symptom_type.description: What treatments work (or don't work) for specific symptom types within a patient's presentation. Captures that some symptoms may be treatment-responsive while others are refractory.
treatment_efficacy_for_symptom_type.research_value: Distinguishing which symptoms respond to which treatments guides clinical decision-making and identifies treatment-resistant symptom clusters.

diurnal_symptom_variation.description: Pattern of symptom severity changing predictably by time of day (morning worse vs. evening better, or vice versa). Important for understanding circadian components of illness and optimizing treatment timing.
diurnal_symptom_variation.research_value: Researchers need to understand circadian symptom patterns to optimize clinical assessments and identify potential underlying mechanisms like immune dysregulation or autonomic dysfunction.

medication_sexual_dysfunction.description: Sexual side effects from medications, particularly antihistamines (H2 blockers like Pepcid/omeprazole) causing loss of sensation or orgasm difficulty. Reported as emerging after starting medication.
medication_sexual_dysfunction.research_value: Medication side effects on sexual function and energy levels are poorly tracked but significantly impact quality of life; documenting these helps optimize medication selection in post-viral conditions.

functional_capacity_decline.description: Documentation of progressive loss of functional ability or new symptom emergence after disease duration, including inability to perform previously tolerated activities (bathing, eating, light exercise).
functional_capacity_decline.research_value: Tracking functional decline trajectories helps characterize disease progression and identify when interventions are needed.

emotional_dissociation_symptoms.description: Depersonalization, derealization, emotional numbness, anhedonia, and detachment from feelings or surroundings
emotional_dissociation_symptoms.research_value: Dissociative and emotional symptoms in Long COVID are frequently misdiagnosed as primary psychiatric conditions, requiring distinct tracking

specialist_type_consulted.description: Specific types of medical specialists patients consulted during diagnostic evaluation
specialist_type_consulted.research_value: Tracking which specialists patients see helps identify diagnostic pathways and specialist awareness of Long COVID

comorbid_mast_cell_reactivity.description: Patient-reported allergic or histamine-related reactions including urticaria, rashes, or adverse reactions to supplements/medications, often occurring in patients with MCAS diagnosis or suspicion.
comorbid_mast_cell_reactivity.research_value: MCAS frequently co-occurs with dysautonomia; documenting reactivity patterns helps optimize treatment selection and identify triggering agents.

symptom_positional_dependency.description: Symptoms that are triggered or worsened by specific body positions or sleeping positions, relevant for understanding postural relationships to symptom exacerbation in conditions like cardiac or neurological long COVID
symptom_positional_dependency.research_value: Helps identify postural triggers that could inform non-pharmacological management and diagnostic patterns in cardiac/neurological presentations

diagnostic_test_discordance.description: Situations where standard diagnostic tests return negative or normal results despite strong clinical symptoms suggesting the condition being tested for.
diagnostic_test_discordance.research_value: Identifying gaps between clinical presentation and standard diagnostic results informs discussions about test sensitivity and non-serologic forms of disease.

supplement_combinations.description: Specific combinations of supplements or intravenous therapies patients report using together, including dosing information when available
supplement_combinations.research_value: Documents patient-reported supplement stacks and their perceived efficacy, useful for understanding self-directed treatment patterns.

post_viral_symptom_emergence.description: New or atypical symptoms appearing during or after long COVID/post-viral illness that represent a change from baseline disease pattern, including symptoms previously absent.
post_viral_symptom_emergence.research_value: Long COVID often presents with evolving or atypical manifestations; tracking new symptom emergence helps characterize disease heterogeneity.
patterns: \b(?:doing too much|overdoing it|over(?:doing|exerting)|my limits?)\s+(?:lately|recently|now|these days)?\s+(?:has|have|is|was|were)?\s*(?:been|become)?\s*(?:just|only|merely|simply)?\s*(?:having a bath|eating lunch|a (?:short|small|little|physio|slow)\s+walk|a shower|basic tasks|getting dressed|sitting up|standing|eating|breathing)\b

\b(?:just|only|merely|simply)\s+(?:having a bath|eating lunch|a (?:short|small|little|physio|slow)\s*walk|getting dressed|a shower|sitting|standing|eating|basic tasks)(?:[,\s]+(?:and|or|eating|drinking|sitting|resting|walking|a \w+ walk))*\s*(?:is|has been|counts as|qualifies as|considered)?\s*(?:too much|my limit|doing too much|overdoing it|exertion|exhausting)\b

quality_of_life_psychological_burden.description: Patient-reported emotional distress, frustration, or psychological impact related to chronic illness progression, functional loss, or disease burden (distinct from diagnosed mental health conditions).
quality_of_life_psychological_burden.research_value: Psychological burden and quality-of-life impact are critical outcomes in chronic illness research and inform holistic treatment approaches.
patterns: (?:i\s+)?(?:miss|missed|missing)\s+(?:my\s+)?(?:life|living|who i was|myself|everything|the person i used to be|being able to|my old self)

gluten_exposure_management_uncertainty.description: Ambiguity in dietary management decisions when celiac or gluten sensitivity diagnosis is uncertain, reflected in inconsistent cross-contamination precautions.
gluten_exposure_management_uncertainty.research_value: Understanding how diagnostic uncertainty affects self-management in celiac and non-celiac gluten sensitivity helps optimize counseling strategies.

medication_adverse_reaction_patterns.description: Unexpected or iatrogenic adverse effects from prescribed medications or supplements that weren't warned about or weren't anticipated by the patient. Captures medication safety issues in chronic illness populations.
medication_adverse_reaction_patterns.research_value: Important for understanding medication safety in chronic illness populations and identifying drug-condition interactions (e.g., potassium supplements in GERD patients).

exertional_symptom_flare.description: Specific patterns where exercise, activity, or exertion triggers symptom exacerbation or flares that persist for days, characteristic of Post-Exertional Malaise (PEM)
exertional_symptom_flare.research_value: Essential for identifying PEM patterns and establishing activity threshold guidelines for chronic illness management

neurological_symptom_pattern.description: Specific neurological manifestations including neuropathy, muscle twitching, facial numbness, and progressive neurological symptoms
neurological_symptom_pattern.research_value: Helps characterize central and peripheral nervous system involvement patterns in post-infectious and post-vaccine conditions

cardiac_rhythm_abnormalities.description: Documentation of specific cardiac arrhythmias or rhythm disturbances (tachycardia, bradycardia, PVCs, PACs) that patients report experiencing
cardiac_rhythm_abnormalities.research_value: Identifies specific arrhythmia patterns in post-COVID and post-vaccine populations that warrant cardiac monitoring

symptom_temporal_pattern.description: Time-based patterns of symptom onset, progression, and duration including acute vs. delayed onset timing relative to triggering events
symptom_temporal_pattern.research_value: Establishes temporal relationships between triggers and symptom clusters, useful for understanding pathophysiology

gi_symptom_disturbance.description: Gastrointestinal symptoms including altered stool characteristics, abdominal distension, and inflammatory indicators
gi_symptom_disturbance.research_value: Identifies GI involvement patterns that may reflect dysbiosis, inflammation, or malabsorption in post-viral conditions
patterns: \b(?:bloat(?:ing|ed)?|distension|distended|swollen belly|swollen abdomen|abdominal swelling|stomach swelling|gut swelling|tummy swelling|belly swelling)\b[^.!?]{0,80}

\b(?:colon|bowel|intestin(?:al|e)|digestive|gi|gastrointestinal|gut)\b[^.!?]{0,60}\b(?:cleanse|clean.?out|flush|purge|intervention|treatment|medication|diet|doesn.?t help|not help(?:ing)?|no relief|unresponsive|still swollen|still bloated|still heavy)

activity_tolerance_modification.description: Changes in functional capacity and adaptations made to work and daily activities due to symptoms
activity_tolerance_modification.research_value: Quantifies functional impairment and identifies workplace accommodations necessary for sustained employment in chronic illness

specialist_diagnostic_evaluation.description: Specific specialists consulted and types of diagnostic testing pursued for symptom evaluation
specialist_diagnostic_evaluation.research_value: Tracks typical diagnostic pathways and specialist involvement patterns in chronic illness evaluation

tissue_wasting_pattern.description: Documentation of progressive loss of muscle mass, connective tissue, or localized wasting in limbs despite continued activity/nutrition
tissue_wasting_pattern.research_value: May indicate myopathic or systemic degenerative processes requiring neuromuscular and rheumatologic investigation

prior_recovery_level.description: The percentage or descriptive baseline functional capacity patients achieve between illness episodes, which may improve, stabilize, or deteriorate over time
prior_recovery_level.research_value: Tracking recovery percentages helps researchers assess whether chronic conditions follow progressive decline patterns or stable remission-relapse cycles

assessment_symptom_overlap_confusion.description: Patient experiences or reports where standardized diagnostic questionnaires (depression/anxiety scales) misattribute organic disease symptoms to psychiatric conditions, creating diagnostic confusion.
assessment_symptom_overlap_confusion.research_value: Documenting symptom-screening tool misalignment helps improve diagnostic processes and prevents unnecessary psychiatric treatment in organic disease.

post_exertional_decline.description: Deterioration in performance or function during prolonged activity, specifically tasks that start well but degrade over sustained effort. Captures the pattern of initial capacity followed by decline.
post_exertional_decline.research_value: Post-exertional decline is a hallmark of ME/CFS and related post-viral conditions; tracking this pattern helps confirm diagnosis and severity.

test_discordance_with_symptoms.description: Situations where diagnostic testing (EMG, EKG, echo, cardiac MRI) returns normal or near-normal results despite significant active symptoms
test_discordance_with_symptoms.research_value: Critical for understanding why standard testing may fail to detect pathology in chronic post-viral/post-vaccine conditions

symptom_clustering.description: Co-occurrence of multiple symptoms occurring together in identifiable clusters or patterns, such as vision problems with brain fog, or specific activity-related symptom combinations.
symptom_clustering.research_value: Identifying symptom clusters helps differentiate disease subtypes and may reveal underlying shared pathophysiology.

vision_problems_undiagnosed.description: Visual disturbances including focus issues, floaters, and eye coordination problems that persist despite normal eye exam results. Captures the mismatch between subjective vision symptoms and objective findings.
vision_problems_undiagnosed.research_value: Functional vision problems in post-viral syndromes despite normal ophthalmologic exams suggest central nervous system involvement and are important quality-of-life metrics.

functional_capacity_baseline_shift.description: Changes in the baseline level of functioning or capacity over time, where the patient's previously stable baseline worsens or improves.
functional_capacity_baseline_shift.research_value: Documenting baseline shifts helps track disease progression and identify critical thresholds where interventions may be needed.

recovery_trajectory_percentage.description: Patient-reported percentage improvement or functional recovery at timepoints during illness course. Provides quantifiable measure of symptom burden reduction over time.
recovery_trajectory_percentage.research_value: Quantifies functional improvement over disease course to characterize recovery patterns and identify prognostic factors.

post_viral_syndrome.description: Persistent, multi-system symptoms following acute viral infection (fever, fatigue, autonomic dysfunction, exertional intolerance). Captures long COVID and similar post-infectious conditions with characteristic symptom clusters.
post_viral_syndrome.research_value: Captures distinctive post-viral illness phenotypes with multi-system involvement and autonomic features for comparative disease mechanisms research.

medication_trial_sequence.description: Chronological sequence of medications attempted, their timing in disease course, observed efficacy, and reasons for changes or discontinuation.
medication_trial_sequence.research_value: Maps therapeutic strategies over disease course to identify medication efficacy patterns and optimal timing for interventions.

activity_modification_strategy.description: Specific behavioral adaptations and graded activity protocols used during illness recovery, including exercise limitations, household task retraining, and return-to-activity sequencing.
activity_modification_strategy.research_value: Documents specific activity-pacing strategies that correlate with recovery, essential for developing rehabilitation protocols.

dose_response_pattern.description: Nonlinear medication responses, including therapeutic windows, paradoxical worsening at higher doses, or loss of efficacy with dose escalation. Critical for optimizing treatment in conditions with atypical pharmacodynamics.
dose_response_pattern.research_value: Identifies non-standard dose-response relationships that challenge conventional titration strategies and suggest individual pharmacogenetic factors.
